sentences.chinese: 季布，楚人也，為任俠有名。
sentences.literal: Ji Bu was a man of Chu; for acting as a knight-errant (renxia) he had a name.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu, a native of Chu, was celebrated as a knight-errant who lived by a strict code of honor.

sentences.chinese: 項籍使將兵，數窘漢王。
sentences.literal: Xiang Ji had him lead troops; he repeatedly placed the King of Han in straits.
sentences.idiomatic: Xiang Ji put him in command of troops, and he several times drove the King of Han into desperate straits.

sentences.chinese: 項籍滅，高祖購求布千金，敢有舍匿，罪三族。
sentences.literal: When Xiang Ji was destroyed, Gaozu offered a thousand in gold for seeking Bu; whoever dared harbor or conceal him, crime extended to three clans.
sentences.idiomatic: After Xiang Ji fell, Gaozu offered a thousand pieces of gold for Ji Bu's capture and decreed that anyone who dared shelter him would suffer the punishment of kin extermination to the third degree.

sentences.chinese: 布匿濮陽周氏，周氏曰：「漢求將軍急，跡且至臣家，能聽臣，臣敢進計；
sentences.literal: Bu hid with the Zhou clan of Puyang. The Zhou clan said, "Han seeks the general urgently; the trail will soon reach my house; if you can listen to your servant, your servant dares advance a plan;"
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu hid with the Zhou family of Puyang. They said to him, "The Han court is hunting you relentlessly; their agents will soon trace you here. If you will hear me out, I have a plan to offer;"

sentences.chinese: 即否，願先自剄。」
sentences.literal: "If not, I wish first to cut my own throat."
sentences.idiomatic: "If you refuse, I would rather cut my own throat first."

sentences.chinese: 布許之。
sentences.literal: Bu assented to it.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu agreed.

sentences.chinese: 乃髡鉗布，衣褐，置廣柳車中，并與其家僮數十人，之魯朱家所賣之。
sentences.literal: Thereupon they shaved Bu's head and applied the iron collar, clothed him in coarse cloth, placed him in a broad-willow cart, together with several tens of their household servants, and went to the place of Zhu Jia of Lu to sell him.
sentences.idiomatic: They shaved Ji Bu's head, fitted him with the convict's collar, dressed him in a laborer's hemp robe, stowed him in a freight cart among dozens of household slaves, and drove him to Zhu Jia's estate in Lu to be sold off as a slave.

sentences.chinese: 朱家心知其季布也，買置田舍。
sentences.literal: Zhu Jia in his heart knew that he was Ji Bu; he bought him and placed him in a field dwelling.
sentences.idiomatic: Zhu Jia recognized him at once as Ji Bu, purchased him, and lodged him on a country farm.

sentences.chinese: 乃之雒陽見汝陰侯滕公，說曰：「季布何罪？
sentences.literal: Thereupon he went to Luoyang to see the Marquis of Ruyin, Lord Teng, and urged, saying, "What crime has Ji Bu?"
sentences.idiomatic: He then traveled to Luoyang to see the Marquis of Ruyin, Lord Teng, and argued, "What wrong has Ji Bu committed?"

sentences.chinese: 臣各為其主用，職耳。
sentences.literal: Ministers each are used for their lord; it is duty only.
sentences.idiomatic: Each man serves his own lord as duty requires—that is all there is to it.

sentences.chinese: 項氏臣豈可盡誅邪？
sentences.literal: Could the ministers of the Xiang clan all be executed?
sentences.idiomatic: Surely you cannot execute every minister who ever served the house of Xiang.

sentences.chinese: 今上始得天下，而以私怨求一人，何示不廣也！
sentences.literal: Now the Sovereign has just obtained the world, yet by private resentment seeks one man—what a showing of not being broad!
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor has only just won the empire; to hound one man out of private spite hardly shows a generous breadth of mind.

sentences.chinese: 且以季布之賢，漢求之急如此，此不北走胡，南走越耳。
sentences.literal: Moreover, by Ji Bu's worthiness, with Han seeking him with urgency such as this, this is nothing but fleeing north to the Hu or fleeing south to Yue.
sentences.idiomatic: A man of Ji Bu's caliber, pressed this hard by the Han, will simply flee north to the steppe peoples or south into the Yue lands.

sentences.chinese: 夫忌壯士以資敵國，此伍子胥所以鞭荊平之墓也。
sentences.literal: As for fearing brave men so as to supply the enemy state—this is the reason Wu Zixu whipped King Ping of Jing's tomb.
sentences.idiomatic: To drive bold men into the arms of rival powers out of fear—think of why Wu Zixu lashed the grave of King Ping of Chu.

sentences.chinese: 君何不從容為上言之？」
sentences.literal: Why do you not, at ease, speak of it to the Sovereign for him?"
sentences.idiomatic: Why not find a tactful moment to put this to the emperor yourself?"

sentences.chinese: 滕公心知朱家大俠，意布匿其所，乃許諾。
sentences.literal: Lord Teng in his heart knew Zhu Jia was a great knight-errant; he suspected Bu was hidden at his place; thereupon he promised.
sentences.idiomatic: Lord Teng knew Zhu Jia by reputation as a great knight-errant and guessed that Ji Bu lay concealed there; he agreed to plead his case.

sentences.chinese: 侍間，果言如朱家指。
sentences.literal: Waiting for an interval, he indeed spoke as Zhu Jia had directed.
sentences.idiomatic: He waited for a suitable opening and repeated Zhu Jia's arguments to the throne.

sentences.chinese: 上乃赦布。
sentences.literal: The Sovereign thereupon pardoned Bu.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor then pardoned Ji Bu.

sentences.chinese: 當是時，諸公皆多布能摧剛為柔，朱家亦以此名聞當世。
sentences.literal: At that time, the various nobles all praised Bu for being able to grind rigid into soft; Zhu Jia also by this had his name heard in the age.
sentences.idiomatic: At the time, leading men admired Ji Bu for turning inflexible danger into a yielding escape, and Zhu Jia's name rang through the age for what he had done.

sentences.chinese: 布召見，謝，拜郎中。
sentences.literal: Bu was summoned for audience, thanked, and appointed gentleman of the palace.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu was summoned to court, expressed his gratitude, and was appointed a gentleman-attendant.

sentences.chinese: 孝惠時，為中郎將。
sentences.literal: In the time of Emperor Xiaohui, he became central gentleman-general.
sentences.idiomatic: Under Emperor Xiaohui he rose to the rank of Commandant of the Palace Gentlemen.

sentences.chinese: 單于嘗為書嫚呂太后，太后怒，召諸將議之。
sentences.literal: The Chanyu once composed a letter insulting Empress Dowager Lü; the empress dowager was angry and summoned the various generals to deliberate on it.
sentences.idiomatic: The Xiongnu chanyu had once sent a letter grossly insulting Empress Dowager Lü. Enraged, she convened her generals to discuss a military response.

sentences.chinese: 上將軍樊噲曰：「臣願得十萬眾，橫行匈奴中。」
sentences.literal: Senior General Fan Kuai said, "Your servant wishes to obtain a hundred thousand multitudes and sweep across within the Xiongnu."
sentences.idiomatic: Fan Kuai, the senior general, declared, "Give me a hundred thousand men and I will sweep through the heart of the Xiongnu domain."

sentences.chinese: 諸將皆阿呂太后，以噲言為然。
sentences.literal: The various generals all fawned on Empress Dowager Lü and took Kuai's words as correct.
sentences.idiomatic: The generals all flattered the empress dowager and agreed that Fan Kuai was right.

sentences.chinese: 布曰：「樊噲可斬也。
sentences.literal: Bu said, "Fan Kuai may be executed.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu said, "Fan Kuai deserves execution.

sentences.chinese: 夫以高帝兵三十餘萬，困於平城，噲時亦在其中。
sentences.literal: For with Gaodi's troops thirty-plus myriads, trapped at Pingcheng, Kuai at that time also was within them.
sentences.idiomatic: Gaozu once led more than three hundred thousand men and was still trapped at Pingcheng; Fan Kuai was in that army.

sentences.chinese: 今噲奈何以十萬眾橫行匈奴中，面謾！
sentences.literal: Now how does Kuai rely on a hundred thousand multitudes to sweep across within the Xiongnu—to the face deceive!
sentences.idiomatic: How can he now claim he will rampage through Xiongnu territory with a mere hundred thousand? He is lying to your faces!

sentences.chinese: 且秦以事胡，陳勝等起。
sentences.literal: Moreover, Qin by serving the Hu, Chen Sheng and others rose.
sentences.idiomatic: Besides, the Qin exhausted the realm on campaigns against the northern tribes, and men like Chen Sheng rose in rebellion.

sentences.chinese: 今瘡痍未瘳，噲又面諛，欲搖動天下。」
sentences.literal: Now wounds and sores not yet healed, Kuai again flatters to the face, wishing to shake and move the world."
sentences.idiomatic: Our wounds from war are still raw, and Fan Kuai would flatter you to your face and risk unsettling the whole empire."

sentences.chinese: 是時殿上皆恐，太后罷朝，遂不復議擊匈奴事。
sentences.literal: At that time those on the hall all feared; the empress dowager dismissed court; thereupon they did not again deliberate on the matter of striking the Xiongnu.
sentences.idiomatic: A chill fell over the hall; the empress dowager adjourned court, and the proposal to attack the Xiongnu was never revived.

sentences.chinese: 布為河東守。
sentences.literal: Bu became Hedong governor.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu was appointed governor of Hedong.

sentences.chinese: 孝文時，人有言其賢，召欲以為御史大夫。
sentences.literal: In the time of Emperor Xiaowen, a person spoke of his worthiness; he was summoned with the desire to make him imperial counselor.
sentences.idiomatic: Under Emperor Xiaowen, someone praised his abilities; the emperor summoned him with the intention of appointing him imperial counselor.

sentences.chinese: 人又言其勇，使酒難近。
sentences.literal: A person again spoke of his braveness, letting wine, difficult to approach.
sentences.idiomatic: Others warned that he was headstrong and surly when drunk, and hard to work with at court.

sentences.chinese: 至，留邸一月，見罷。
sentences.literal: When he arrived, retained in the lodging one month, dismissed upon audience.
sentences.idiomatic: When he arrived, he was kept at the official lodge for a month and then dismissed without receiving an appointment.

sentences.chinese: 布進曰：「臣待罪河東，陛下無故召臣，此人必有以臣欺陛下者。
sentences.literal: Bu advanced and said, "Your servant awaits offense at Hedong; Your Majesty without cause summoned your servant—this person must have had something by your servant to deceive Your Majesty.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu stepped forward and said, "I stand under sentence of duty at Hedong; Your Majesty summoned me without apparent reason—someone must have used me to mislead you."

sentences.chinese: 今臣至，無所受事，罷去，此人必有毀臣者。
sentences.literal: Now your servant has arrived, receiving no affairs, dismissed to go—this person must have had one who slanders your servant.
sentences.idiomatic: Now that I have come, I have been given no assignment and am sent away—someone must be slandering me behind my back."

sentences.chinese: 夫陛下以一人譽召臣，一人毀去臣，臣恐天下有識者聞之，有以窺陛下。」
sentences.literal: As for Your Majesty by one man's praise summoning your servant, by one man's slander dismissing your servant, your servant fears those in the world with knowledge who hear it will have something by which to peer into Your Majesty."
sentences.idiomatic: If a single man's praise brings me to court and a single man's slander sends me away, thoughtful men everywhere will draw their own conclusions about how Your Majesty judges men."

sentences.chinese: 上默然，慚曰：「河東吾股肱郡，故特召君耳。」
sentences.literal: The Sovereign was silent, ashamedly said, "Hedong is my thigh-arm county; therefore I specially summoned you."
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor sat in silence, then said with embarrassment, "Hedong is a pillar of the realm; I summoned you for that reason alone."

sentences.chinese: 布之官。
sentences.literal: Bu went to his office.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu returned to his post.

sentences.chinese: 辯士曹丘生數招權顧金錢，事貴人趙談等，與竇長君善。
sentences.literal: The rhetorician Cao Qiusheng several times summoned power and looked to gold and money, served noble persons Zhao Tan and others, and was good with Dou Changjun.
sentences.idiomatic: Cao Qiusheng, a persuasive talker, repeatedly traded on influence for cash, attached himself to powerful men such as Zhao Tan, and was on close terms with Dou Changjun.

sentences.chinese: 布聞，寄書諫長君曰：「吾聞曹丘生非長者，勿與通。」
sentences.literal: Bu heard, sent a letter remonstrating Changjun, saying, "I have heard Cao Qiusheng is not an elder; do not communicate with him."
sentences.idiomatic: When Ji Bu heard of it, he wrote Dou Changjun a warning: "Cao Qiusheng is no man of integrity. Have nothing to do with him."

sentences.chinese: 及曹丘生歸，欲得書請布。
sentences.literal: When Cao Qiusheng returned, he wished to obtain a letter to request Bu.
sentences.idiomatic: When Cao Qiusheng returned home, he asked for a letter of introduction to Ji Bu.

sentences.chinese: 竇長君曰：「季將軍不說足下，足下無往。」
sentences.literal: Dou Changjun said, "General Ji is not pleased with your foot; your foot has no going."
sentences.idiomatic: Dou Changjun replied, "General Ji has no liking for you. You had better not go."

sentences.chinese: 固請書，遂行。
sentences.literal: He firmly requested the letter; thereupon he went.
sentences.idiomatic: Cao pressed his request until he obtained the letter and set out.

sentences.chinese: 使人先發書，布果大怒，待曹丘。
sentences.literal: He sent a man first to dispatch the letter; Bu indeed was greatly angry, awaiting Cao Qiu.
sentences.idiomatic: He had his letter of introduction sent ahead; Ji Bu was furious and waited for Cao Qiusheng.

sentences.chinese: 曹丘至，則揖布曰：「楚人諺曰『得黃金百，不如得季布諾』，足下何以得此聲梁楚之間哉？
sentences.literal: When Cao Qiu arrived, then he bowed to Bu and said, "The Chu people have a proverb, 'Obtaining a hundred in yellow gold is not as good as obtaining Ji Bu's promise'—how did your foot obtain this reputation between Liang and Chu?"
sentences.idiomatic: When Cao Qiusheng arrived, he bowed to Ji Bu and said, "The people of Chu say, 'A hundred pounds of gold cannot buy Ji Bu's word.' How did you earn such a name between Liang and Chu?"

sentences.chinese: 且僕與足下俱楚人，使僕游揚足下名於天下，顧不美乎？
sentences.literal: Moreover, your servant and your foot are both Chu men; if your servant travels to raise your foot's name in the world, would it not be beautiful?"
sentences.idiomatic: We are both men of Chu; if I speak well of you to the world, would that not serve you handsomely?"

sentences.chinese: 何足下距僕之深也！」
sentences.literal: Why does your foot reject your servant so deeply!"
sentences.idiomatic: Yet you have shut me out so coldly!"

sentences.chinese: 布乃大說。
sentences.literal: Bu thereupon was greatly pleased.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu was completely won over.

sentences.chinese: 引入，留數月，為上客，厚送之。
sentences.literal: He led him in, retained several months, made him upper guest, richly sent him off.
sentences.idiomatic: He brought him inside, kept him for months as an honored guest, and sent him away with rich gifts.

sentences.chinese: 布名所以益聞者，曹丘揚之也。
sentences.literal: The reason Bu's name became increasingly heard was Cao Qiu raised it.
sentences.idiomatic: It was Cao Qiusheng's praise that spread Ji Bu's fame still farther.

sentences.chinese: 布弟季心氣蓋關中，遇人恭謹，為任俠，方數千里，士爭為死。
sentences.literal: Bu's younger brother Ji Xin spirit covered Guanzhong; meeting people respectful and careful, acting as knight-errant, territory several thousand li, gentlemen strove to die for him.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu's younger brother Ji Xin dominated Guanzhong in sheer dash; though unfailingly courteous in meeting others, he was a knight-errant whose reputation carried for thousands of li, and fighting men vied to die for him.

sentences.chinese: 嘗殺人，亡吳，從爰絲匿，長事爰絲，弟畜灌夫、籍福之屬。
sentences.literal: He once killed a man, fled to Wu, followed Yuansi hiding, elder-served Yuansi, younger-brother-raised Guan Fu, Ji Fu's class.
sentences.idiomatic: He once killed a man and fled to Wu, where he hid with Yuan Ang, served Yuan as an elder brother would be served, and treated the likes of Guan Fu and Ji Fu as younger companions.

sentences.chinese: 嘗為中司馬，中尉郅都不敢加。
sentences.literal: He once was central sima; Central Commandant Zhi Du did not dare apply.
sentences.idiomatic: When he served as an aide in the central command, even the dreaded Zhi Du dared not touch him.

sentences.chinese: 少年多時時竊借其名以行。
sentences.literal: Youths many times secretly borrowed his name to act.
sentences.idiomatic: Young blades often falsely invoked his name.

sentences.chinese: 當是時，季心以勇，布以諾，聞關中。
sentences.literal: At that time, Ji Xin by bravery, Bu by promise, were heard in Guanzhong.
sentences.idiomatic: At the time Ji Xin was known for valor and Ji Bu for keeping his word; both names filled Guanzhong.

sentences.chinese: 布母弟丁公，為項羽將，逐窘高祖彭城西。
sentences.literal: Bu's full younger brother Lord Ding was a general for Xiang Yu, pursuing and placing Gaozu in straits west of Pengcheng.
sentences.idiomatic: Ji Bu's younger uterine brother, Lord Ding, served as a general under Xiang Yu and drove Gaozu into desperate straits west of Pengcheng.

sentences.chinese: 短兵接，漢王急，顧謂丁公曰：「兩賢豈相厄哉！」
sentences.literal: Short weapons engaged; the King of Han was urgent; looking back he said to Lord Ding, "Two worthies—how could they mutually distress!"
sentences.idiomatic: In the melee the King of Han was hard pressed; turning to Lord Ding he cried, "Surely two able men need not ruin each other!"

sentences.chinese: 丁公引兵而還。
sentences.literal: Lord Ding led troops and returned.
sentences.idiomatic: Lord Ding withdrew his troops and let him go.

sentences.chinese: 及項王滅，丁公謁見高祖，以丁公徇軍中，曰：「丁公為項王臣不忠，使項王失天下者也。」
sentences.literal: When King Xiang was destroyed, Lord Ding requested audience with Gaozu; using Lord Ding to parade in the army, he said, "Lord Ding as King Xiang's minister was not loyal; he is the one who made King Xiang lose the world."
sentences.idiomatic: After Xiang Yu fell, Lord Ding presented himself to Gaozu. Gaozu had him paraded before the troops and declared, "Lord Ding was a faithless minister to the King of Xiang; he is the man who cost Xiang Yu the empire."

sentences.chinese: 遂斬之，曰：「使後為人臣無傚丁公也！」
sentences.literal: Thereupon he executed him, saying, "Cause those after who are ministers for others not to imitate Lord Ding!"
sentences.idiomatic: He had him beheaded, saying, "Let no minister hereafter take Lord Ding as his model!"

sentences.chinese: 欒布，梁人也。
sentences.literal: Luan Bu was a man of Liang.
sentences.idiomatic: Luan Bu was a native of Liang.

sentences.chinese: 彭越為家人時，嘗與布游，窮困，賣庸於齊，為酒家保。
sentences.literal: When Peng Yue was a commoner, he once roamed with Bu; in poverty and distress, he sold hireling labor in Qi and became a tavern guarantor.
sentences.idiomatic: When Peng Yue was still a commoner, he had roamed with Luan Bu in hardship; destitute, they hired out as laborers in Qi, and Luan Bu worked as a bondservant in a wineshop.

sentences.chinese: 數歲別去，而布為人所略，賣為奴於燕。
sentences.literal: Several years they parted and went; but Bu was taken by persons in raid and sold as a slave in Yan.
sentences.idiomatic: After some years they parted, and Luan Bu was kidnapped and sold into slavery in Yan.

sentences.chinese: 為其主家報仇，燕將臧荼舉以為都尉。
sentences.literal: For his master's household he avenged a wrong; Yan general Zang Tu raised him and made him commandant.
sentences.idiomatic: He avenged his master's family on their enemies, and the Yan general Zang Tu thereupon appointed him a commandant.

sentences.chinese: 荼為燕王，布為將。
sentences.literal: Tu became King of Yan; Bu became general.
sentences.idiomatic: When Zang Tu became King of Yan, Luan Bu served as one of his generals.

sentences.chinese: 及荼反，漢擊燕，虜布。
sentences.literal: When Tu rebelled, Han struck Yan and captured Bu.
sentences.idiomatic: When Zang Tu rebelled, the Han attacked Yan and took Luan Bu prisoner.

sentences.chinese: 梁王彭越聞之，乃言上，請贖布為梁大夫。
sentences.literal: King of Liang Peng Yue heard it; thereupon he spoke to the Sovereign, requesting to ransom Bu to be Liang grandee.
sentences.idiomatic: King Peng Yue of Liang heard of it and petitioned the throne to ransom Luan Bu and appoint him a counselor of Liang.

sentences.chinese: 使於齊，未反，漢召彭越責以謀反，夷三族，梟首雒陽，下詔有收視者輒捕之。
sentences.literal: He was sent on mission to Qi; not yet returned, Han summoned Peng Yue and reproached him with plotting rebellion, exterminated three clans, displayed the head at Luoyang, and issued an edict that whoever there was who collected or viewed would immediately be arrested.
sentences.idiomatic: He was on a mission to Qi and had not returned when the Han summoned Peng Yue, charged him with treason, wiped out three generations of his kin, exposed his head at Luoyang, and decreed that anyone who dared mourn or bury him would be arrested at once.

sentences.chinese: 布還，奏事彭越頭下，祠而哭之。
sentences.literal: Bu returned, reported affairs beneath Peng Yue's head, sacrificed and wept for him.
sentences.idiomatic: When Luan Bu returned, he presented his report beneath Peng Yue's impaled head, then offered sacrifice and wept for his lord.

sentences.chinese: 吏捕以聞。
sentences.literal: Officials arrested and reported.
sentences.idiomatic: The authorities seized him and reported the matter.

sentences.chinese: 上召布罵曰：「若與彭越反邪？
sentences.literal: The Sovereign summoned Bu and reviled, saying, "You with Peng Yue rebelled?"
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor summoned Luan Bu and shouted, "Were you in league with Peng Yue?"

sentences.chinese: 吾禁人勿收，若獨祠而哭之，與反明矣。
sentences.literal: I forbade people not to collect; you alone sacrificed and wept for him—with rebellion it is clear.
sentences.idiomatic: I forbade anyone to bury him; you alone mourned him with sacrifice and tears—your guilt is plain as rebellion."

sentences.chinese: 趣亨之。」
sentences.literal: "Hasten to boil him."
sentences.idiomatic: "Boil him at once."

sentences.chinese: 方提趨湯，顧曰：「願一言而死。」
sentences.literal: Just as he was raised and hurried toward the cauldron, looking back he said, "I wish one word then die."
sentences.idiomatic: As they dragged him toward the boiling cauldron, he turned and said, "Let me speak one sentence before I die."

sentences.chinese: 上曰：「何言？」
sentences.literal: The Sovereign said, "What words?"
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor asked, "What is it?"

sentences.chinese: 布曰：「方上之困彭城，敗滎陽、成皋間，項王所以不能遂西，徒以彭王居梁地，與漢合從苦楚也。
sentences.literal: Bu said, "When the Sovereign was trapped at Pengcheng, defeated between Xingyang and Chenggao, the reason King Xiang could not thereupon go west was solely because the King of Peng dwelt in Liang territory, with Han joining staffs to distress Chu.
sentences.idiomatic: Luan Bu said, "When Your Majesty was trapped at Pengcheng and driven back between Xingyang and Chenggao, the reason Xiang Yu could not push west was that the King of Peng held Liang and allied with Han to pin Chu in a vise."

sentences.chinese: 當是之時，彭王壹顧，與楚則漢破，與漢則楚破。
sentences.literal: At that time, if the King of Peng had one look—if with Chu then Han was broken; if with Han then Chu was broken.
sentences.idiomatic: In those days a single shift of Peng Yue's weight—if he had thrown in with Chu, Han would have shattered; if with Han, Chu would have shattered."

sentences.chinese: 且垓下之會，微彭王，項氏不亡。
sentences.literal: Moreover, at the meeting of Gaixia, without the King of Peng, the Xiang clan would not have perished.
sentences.idiomatic: At Gaixia, had it not been for the King of Peng, the house of Xiang would not have fallen."

sentences.chinese: 天下已定，彭王剖符受封，亦欲傳之萬世。
sentences.literal: The world already settled, the King of Peng split tallies and received enfeoffment; he also wished to transmit it ten thousand generations.
sentences.idiomatic: Once the realm was settled, the King of Peng received his investiture on split tallies like any other lord; he meant his line to endure for ages."

sentences.chinese: 今漢壹徵兵於梁，彭王病不行，而疑以為反。
sentences.literal: Now Han once summoned troops from Liang; the King of Peng was ill and did not go, yet was suspected and deemed to have rebelled.
sentences.idiomatic: Now a single levy of troops from Liang found the king too ill to march, and on that slender ground he was branded a traitor."

sentences.chinese: 反形未見，以苛細誅之，臣恐功臣人人自危也。
sentences.literal: Rebellion form not yet seen; by harsh minuteness executing him, your servant fears merit ministers each man will self-fear.
sentences.idiomatic: No act of rebellion had appeared; to put him to death on petty pretexts will make every man who helped win the empire fear for his life."

sentences.chinese: 今彭王已死，臣生不如死，請就亨。」
sentences.literal: Now the King of Peng is already dead; your servant alive is not as good as dead; I request to approach the boiling."
sentences.idiomatic: Peng Yue is dead; for me to live on would be worse than death. Send me to the cauldron."

sentences.chinese: 上乃釋布，拜為都尉。
sentences.literal: The Sovereign thereupon released Bu and appointed him commandant.
sentences.idiomatic: The emperor then spared Luan Bu and appointed him a commandant.

sentences.chinese: 孝文時，為燕相，至將軍。
sentences.literal: In the time of Emperor Xiaowen, he became Yan chancellor, reaching general.
sentences.idiomatic: Under Emperor Xiaowen he served as chancellor of Yan and rose to the rank of general.

sentences.chinese: 布稱曰：「窮困不能辱身，非人也；
sentences.literal: Bu declared, saying, "In poverty and distress unable to humiliate the body—not a person;"
sentences.idiomatic: Luan Bu used to say, "In poverty, if you cannot endure indignity to the body, you are not a true man;"

sentences.chinese: 富貴不能快意，非賢也。」
sentences.literal: "In wealth and honor unable to satisfy the will—not worthy."
sentences.idiomatic: "and in wealth and rank, if you cannot live as your heart dictates, you are no worthy man."

sentences.chinese: 於是嘗有德，厚報之；
sentences.literal: Thereupon when there had been one who showed him kindness, he richly repaid him;
sentences.idiomatic: Those who had done him a kindness he repaid with lavish generosity;

sentences.chinese: 有怨，必以法滅之。
sentences.literal: when there was resentment, he invariably by law destroyed them.
sentences.idiomatic: those who had wronged him he destroyed to the letter of the law.

sentences.chinese: 吳楚反時，以功封為鄃侯，復為燕相。
sentences.literal: When Wu and Chu rebelled, by merit he was enfeoffed as Marquis of Yu and again became Yan chancellor.
sentences.idiomatic: During the rebellion of Wu and Chu he was enfeoffed as Marquis of Yu for his service and again became chancellor of Yan.

sentences.chinese: 燕齊之間皆為立社，號曰欒公社。
sentences.literal: Between Yan and Qi all for him established altars of the soil, called Luan Duke's altars.
sentences.idiomatic: Between Yan and Qi the people founded shrines to him and called them the altars of Duke Luan.

sentences.chinese: 布薨，子賁嗣侯，孝武時坐為太常犧牲不如令，國除。
sentences.literal: Bu passed away; his son Ben inherited the marquisate; in the time of Emperor Xiaowu he sat for as Grand Master of Ceremonies the sacrificial victims not according to ordinance; the state was removed.
sentences.idiomatic: When Luan Bu died, his son Ben inherited the marquisate; under Emperor Wu he lost the fief for supplying sacrificial animals to the Grand Master of Ceremonies that failed to meet regulations.

sentences.chinese: 田叔，趙陘城人也。
sentences.literal: Tian Shu was a man of Xingcheng in Zhao.
sentences.idiomatic: Tian Shu was a native of Xingcheng in Zhao.

sentences.chinese: 其先，齊田氏也。
sentences.literal: His ancestors were the Tian clan of Qi.
sentences.idiomatic: His family traced its descent to the Tian house of Qi.

sentences.chinese: 叔好劍，學黃老術於樂鉅公。
sentences.literal: Shu liked swords; he studied Huang-Lao arts with Lord Yue Ju.
sentences.idiomatic: Tian Shu loved swordsmanship and studied Huang-Lao doctrine under Master Yue Ju.

sentences.chinese: 為人廉直，喜任俠。
sentences.literal: As a person incorrupt and straight, he delighted in knight-errantry.
sentences.idiomatic: He was scrupulous and upright, and delighted in the code of the knight-errant.

sentences.chinese: 游諸公，趙人舉之趙相趙午，言之趙王張敖，以為郎中。
sentences.literal: He roamed among the nobles; men of Zhao raised him to Zhao Chancellor Zhao Wu, who spoke of him to King of Zhao Zhang Ao, and he was made gentleman of the palace.
sentences.idiomatic: He moved among the great families of the capital until men of Zhao recommended him to the Zhao chancellor Zhao Wu, who in turn spoke to King Zhang Ao of Zhao, who appointed him a gentleman-attendant.

sentences.chinese: 數歲，趙王賢之，未及遷。
sentences.literal: Several years, the King of Zhao deemed him worthy; not yet had he been promoted.
sentences.idiomatic: For several years the king of Zhao admired his abilities but had not yet promoted him.

sentences.chinese: 會趙午、貫高等謀弒上，事發覺，漢下詔捕趙王及群臣反者。
sentences.literal: It happened that Zhao Wu, Guan Gao and others plotted to assassinate the Sovereign; the affair was discovered; Han issued an edict to arrest the King of Zhao and the various ministers who were rebels.
sentences.idiomatic: Then Zhao Wu, Guan Gao, and others plotted regicide; when the plot was uncovered, the Han court ordered the arrest of the king of Zhao and every minister implicated in treason.

sentences.chinese: 趙有敢隨王，罪三族。
sentences.literal: In Zhao whoever dared follow the king, crime to three clans.
sentences.idiomatic: In Zhao, anyone who dared follow the king would suffer kin extermination to the third degree.
